safeguarding.active_options_desc: Safeguarding options that are currently active and available to members
safeguarding.behavioral_triggers_desc: Configure automatic flags triggered by specific member behaviours
safeguarding.deactivate_option_desc: Are you sure you want to deactivate this safeguarding option?
safeguarding.desc_guardian_monitors_messages: The guardian can view messages sent to and from this member
safeguarding.desc_help_url: A link to help documentation for this safeguarding option
safeguarding.desc_option_key: A unique identifier for this safeguarding option
safeguarding.desc_required_vetting_type: The vetting type required before this safeguarding option can be assigned
safeguarding.desc_the_vulnerable_user_who_needs_overs: The member who needs oversight from the assigned guardian

help.title: How safeguarding works here
help.subtitle: A guide to the flows you see on this dashboard - flagging, activations, member autonomy, audit. Expand any section for detail.

about.title: About this dashboard
about.intro: The safeguarding dashboard is your view of every member the system is actively protecting. It surfaces:
about.flagged_messages_body: - copies of messages sent or received by monitored members.
about.guardian_assignments_body: - relationships where a coordinator mediates contact for a ward.
about.member_preferences_body: - which safeguarding options each flagged member has selected.
about.live_counts: The numbers at the top are live - the red Safeguarding item in the sidebar shows the count of unreviewed critical flags so you can spot when attention is needed without opening the page.

flagging.title: How members get flagged
flagging.intro: Flags are never applied silently behind the member's back. There are two routes:
flagging.self_identification_prefix: The member ticked one or more options on the safeguarding step during sign-up (e.g.
flagging.example_vulnerable: I consider myself a vulnerable adult
flagging.example_vetted: I prefer only to interact with vetted members
flagging.self_identification_suffix: The system reads the triggers attached to each selected option and OR-merges them into a single set of protections written to
flagging.self_revoke_prefix: Because these are self-chosen, the member can revoke any of them from
flagging.self_revoke_suffix: at any time - without needing to ask you.
flagging.manual_monitoring_suffix: in Broker Controls and place a member under oversight with a reason and optional expiry date. Used for compliance reviews, complaint investigations, or probationary periods after an incident.

triggers.title: What each trigger activates
triggers.intro_suffix: ) carries a JSON block of triggers. When a member selects the option, the relevant triggers activate:

effects.notify_admin_on_selection: You get a real-time notification the moment the option is ticked.
effects.requires_broker_approval: All outgoing messages copied for broker review; matches need sign-off.
effects.requires_vetted_interaction: Non-vetted members are hidden from the member's discovery feed.
effects.restricts_matching: Match approval workflow engages - no auto-introductions.
effects.restricts_messaging: Marks the member under monitoring; message copies begin.
effects.vetting_type_required: Specifies which vetting a contacting member must hold, for example garda_vetting.

triggers.merge_note: When a member selects multiple options, triggers OR-merge: the most protective combination wins. Revoking one option may release some protections but keep others active - the system re-evaluates from scratch each time.

vetting.title: How vetting gates interactions
vetting.intro_prefix: When a flagged member requires a specific vetting type, the system enforces it at three separate points - not just as a UI warning, but as a hard block with a specific error code
vetting.discovery_body: - Smart Matching Engine never surfaces flagged members to someone who doesn't hold the required vetting. They are invisible, not greyed out.
vetting.messaging_prefix: - if a sender lacks vetting the recipient requires,
vetting.match_body: - even if a match is manually submitted for approval, it's blocked bidirectionally if either party is flagged and the other lacks the required vetting.
vetting.group_body: - adding a participant fails if the organiser or the participant requires vetting the other doesn't hold.
vetting.staff_bypass_body: admins, tenant admins, brokers, and super admins see the full pool in discovery (for assignment purposes). Messaging, match submission, and group exchange gates still apply to everyone - there is no admin-level bypass for those.

assignments.title: Guardian / ward assignments
assignments.intro: A guardian assignment pairs a ward (the protected member) with a guardian (a trusted coordinator or family member). Use this when a member needs a specific person mediating their interactions, rather than the general broker pool.
assignments.create_prefix: from the Assignments tab or by navigating to
assignments.create_suffix: Both parties receive an email and an in-app bell notification.
assignments.consent_prefix: - the ward must accept; their acceptance is recorded in
assignments.revoke_prefix: - either the admin or the ward can revoke. Revocation is soft (sets
assignments.revoke_suffix: ) so the audit trail is preserved.
assignments.queue_note: Guardian assignments do not replace the broker review queue - monitored messages still flow there. A guardian is an extra trusted layer, not a substitute for platform oversight.

preferences.title: Reading member preferences
preferences.intro_prefix: The Member Preferences tab lists every flagged member alongside the specific options they've selected. This data is
preferences.intro_suffix: under GDPR - treat it with care.
preferences.audit_logged: Every view is audit-logged with your user ID and timestamp.
preferences.not_public: It is never exposed in a member's public profile.
preferences.admin_only: Other members cannot see this tab - access requires an admin-class role.
preferences.confidential: Do not discuss another member's flags with anyone outside the safeguarding team.
preferences.activity_trail: Click a member's row to open their full activity trail - every message copied, every assignment change, every trigger activation, chronologically. The trail is downloadable as CSV for regulator requests or incident reports.

autonomy.title: Adult autonomy principle
autonomy.rule_prefix: An adult who has self-identified as requiring safeguarding protections has the absolute right to view
autonomy.rule_middle: revoke those protections without asking an administrator. Admins
autonomy.grounded_suffix: (and equivalent UK statutory guidance - "Making Safeguarding Personal"). Revoking a flag triggers:
autonomy.trigger_reevaluation: Re-evaluation of triggers -> monitoring/approval requirements may lift.
autonomy.admin_notification: An in-app bell notification to admins/brokers - you are told, but you cannot veto.
autonomy.coercion_note: If you believe revocation was coerced by a third party, the right response is a safeguarding conversation with the member (and if needed, a referral to statutory services) - not re-applying the flag via admin controls.

annual_review.title: Annual review process
annual_review.intro_prefix: On the first of each month, the
annual_review.intro_suffix: command runs automatically. For any preference older than 365 days without a recent review, it:
annual_review.step_reminder: Sends the member an email and bell asking them to review - stamps
annual_review.step_escalate: If no response, notifies admins/brokers via bell + email - stamps
annual_review.escalation_note: The escalation is a prompt for a coordinator to reach out personally - it is not a cue to strip the protection. Silence is not consent to remove safeguards.
annual_review.settings_suffix: counts as a review - the backend stamps
annual_review.settings_tail: automatically when the member opens the page.

audit.title: Audit trail & CSV export
audit.intro_prefix: Every safeguarding-related action is written to
audit.intro_suffix: The per-member activity endpoint combines three sources into one newest-first timeline:
audit.activity_log_body: - option selections, consent revocations, admin views, trigger activations.
audit.message_copies_body: - every message sent or received by the member while monitored.
audit.assignment_history_body: - guardian assignments created or revoked involving the member.
audit.export_suffix: action on the member detail view when you need a file you can attach to an incident report or share with a regulator. The CSV is UTF-8 with a BOM so Excel opens it correctly without prompting for encoding.
